review_question: After the rejected S9F target-only IO-affinity candidate, is the remaining implementation surface specifically post-enqueue steal/search migration before first local poll, and can S9H be constrained to test that surface without weakening HTTP, CONNECT, TLS, mTLS, public client API, libcurl baseline, or fixed SOTA gates?
answer: Yes. The original S9F red diagnostic showed IO-readiness wake locality mismatch in the real HTTPS-over-HTTPS-proxy slow path. The rejected target-only candidate then proved that changing only the enqueue target is insufficient: in the candidate smoke trace all 22/89 mismatches had target_worker_id equal to last_poll_worker_id while next_poll_worker_id differed. Therefore the remaining narrowed surface is not generic wake targeting; it is whether an IO-woken home-worker task can be protected from post-enqueue steal/search until its first local poll. This is a valid S9H candidate surface only if it is guarded by RED/GREEN evidence and then by the unchanged focused and canonical benchmark gates.

source_evidence.role: S9E architecture frontier and fixed gate source
Original IO readiness locality mismatch red diagnostic
Target-only IO-affinity candidate rejection and rollback evidence
Independent review approval for the narrowed gen-003 strategy

schedule_io.finding: ScheduleIO::wake0 records io_readiness and calls waker.wake_by_ref without changing readiness bits. This is the correct origin marker; S9H must not change readiness semantics.

task_wake_path.finding: TaskHandle::wake_by_ref records task_wake_enqueue and schedules the task with lifo=false when the task transitions to scheduling. This is the point where an IO-origin wake can be marked as a home-worker candidate without changing future polling semantics.

enqueue_path.finding: MultiThreadScheduler::enqueue_under_ctx currently enqueues same-runtime non-lifo tasks into the current worker local queue. The rejected target-only candidate modified this surface and was rolled back. S9H must not re-enter this as a target-only enqueue change; it must prove first-local-poll protection after a home-target enqueue.

steal_path.finding: MultiThreadScheduler::dequeue falls through to get_task_from_searching and steal after local/global/driver/wake_yield checks fail. This is the post-enqueue migration surface that can move a task away from its intended home before the home worker polls it.

local_queue_steal.finding: LocalQueue::steal_into steals half of the source queue when src_front_steal equals src_front_real. It has no task-level notion of first-local-poll protection, so a newly home-targeted IO task can be part of a steal batch before its home worker polls it.

original_red_diagnostic.interpretation: The current runtime path has real same-task IO wake locality mismatch. This supported entering S9F but did not identify whether target enqueue or post-enqueue migration was responsible.

target_only_candidate_smoke.interpretation: The target-only patch got the target worker right for every remaining mismatch sample, yet another worker still performed the next poll. This is direct evidence that S9H must test post-enqueue steal/search protection rather than retesting target selection.

invalidated_as_s9h_support: IO-readiness worker-affine enqueue focused matrix as SOTA proof
scheduler_enqueue_io_affine target-worker trace as proof of home-worker stickiness
any target-only IO wake affinity candidate without anti-steal first-local-poll evidence

s9h_candidate_contract.candidate_surface: ylong_runtime IO home-worker stickiness with anti-steal first-local-poll evidence
allowed_implementation_shape: May attach task-local home-worker information only for IO-readiness wake scheduling.
May enqueue IO-woken tasks to the recorded home worker only when the home local queue has capacity and the scheduler is the same runtime.
May mark such a task or queue position as protected until the home worker performs its first local poll or the task is otherwise canceled/finished.
May make steal/search skip protected first-local-poll tasks while still allowing normal local pop, global queue work, cancellation, and later steals.
forbidden_implementation_shape: Do not weaken or bypass Ready bits, ScheduleIO readiness, TLS/OpenSSL polling, CONNECT semantics, proxy TLS/mTLS behavior, request formatting, or public client API.
Do not globally disable stealing, globally reduce worker count, change block_on behavior, change periodic driver checks, or repeat rejected park/yield/sleeper micro-candidates.
Do not change benchmark baseline mechanics or rely on benchmark-only runtime substitution.
Do not treat focused-only results as canonical SOTA evidence.
required_red_green_gate: RED: a unit or trace test must fail on current gen-003 source because an IO-woken home-targeted task can be stolen or next-polled by a non-home worker before first local poll.

GREEN: after the candidate, the same test must prove the protected IO-home task is either first polled by its home worker or remains available to the home worker, while ordinary non-protected tasks remain stealable.
Analyzer/trace gate: runtime trace must distinguish target-worker correctness from first-local-poll protection; target_worker_id alone is insufficient.
Safety gate: existing async_pool queue tests and benchmark harness tests must still pass; any full runtime --lib abort from pre-existing IO-safety tests is not pass evidence.
s9h_candidate_contract.canonical_gate: Unchanged fixed SOTA gate from requirements.control.json: geomean throughput >=2.00 lower CI, every-scenario throughput >=1.50 lower CI, p50 <=0.60, p95 <=0.70, flagship throughput >=3.00 or p95 <=0.50, CPU geomean <=0.80, CPU every-scenario <=1.00, RSS every-scenario <=1.10, errors 0, repetitions >=5 after warmup.

decision.s9h_next_action: Use TDD to implement a narrow IO-home first-local-poll protection candidate in ylong_runtime, starting with a failing test that a home-targeted IO task is not stolen before the home worker polls it.
decision.reason: S9G narrows the implementation surface and defines the next RED/GREEN and benchmark gates. It is not performance causality evidence and cannot satisfy S9H, S10, or the final SOTA claim.
